summary: Provision brand-new ElastiCache caches: a replicated Redis/Valkey group with automatic failover, or a standalone Memcached/Redis cluster. No ElastiCache estate exists yet, so every operation is a first stand-up an engineer reviews before it applies.

operations.title: Provision a new ElastiCache replication group
operations.summary: Request a brand-new replicated Redis/Valkey cache with automatic failover; an engineer reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a replicated cache as a complete specification: engine, node size, replica count, network placement, and encryption. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: replication_group_id
params.label: Replication group identifier
params.help: Lower-case name AWS uses for the cache's endpoint.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: What this cache is for — required by the provider, shown in the AWS console.

params.name: engine
params.label: Engine
params.help: Replication (automatic failover) is only offered for Redis and its Valkey fork.

params.name: engine_version
params.label: Engine version
params.help: Leave blank for the engineer to pin the version.

params.name: node_type
params.label: Node type
params.help: Size class for processor and memory on every node in the group.

params.name: num_cache_clusters
params.label: Node count (primary + replicas)
params.help: 1 disables replication. 2 or more gives at least one replica for automatic failover.

params.name: automatic_failover_enabled
params.label: Automatic failover
params.help: Requires at least 2 nodes. Promotes a replica automatically if the primary fails.

params.name: multi_az_enabled
params.label: Multi-AZ
params.help: Spreads nodes across Availability Zones so a single zone outage does not take down the cache.

params.name: at_rest_encryption_enabled
params.label: Encryption at rest
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: transit_encryption_enabled
params.label: Encryption in transit
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank to use the default AWS-managed key. Pick a key only for a customer-managed CMK.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: Network placement
params.help: Which private subnets the cache lives in. Empty until the estate provisions or imports an ElastiCache subnet group.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the cache's port.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Cache name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this cache holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this cache.

decisions: AUTH token / Redis password rotation goes through the secrets manager — never entered in this portal
Cluster mode (sharding via node_group_configuration) is an engineer decision — this form provisions the common single-shard, multi-replica case
Parameter group tuning and a non-default maintenance/snapshot window are engineer decisions
Log delivery to CloudWatch or Kinesis Firehose is an engineer follow-up after creation

operations.title: Provision a new ElastiCache standalone cluster
operations.summary: Request a brand-new standalone Memcached or single-node Redis/Valkey cache; an engineer reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a standalone cache as a complete specification: engine, node size and count, and network placement. Use this for Memcached (which has no replication) or a single-node cache; a replicated Redis/Valkey group with failover uses the replication-group form instead. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: cluster_id
params.label: Cluster identifier
params.help: Lower-case name AWS uses for the cluster's endpoint.

params.name: engine
params.label: Engine
params.help: Memcached is the classic standalone-cluster use; pick redis or valkey for a single node with no replication.

params.name: engine_version
params.label: Engine version
params.help: Leave blank for the engineer to pin the version.

params.name: node_type
params.label: Node type
params.help: Size class for processor and memory on every node in the cluster.

params.name: num_cache_nodes
params.label: Node count
params.help: Memcached can shard across multiple nodes; a Redis or Valkey standalone cluster must stay at 1.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: Network placement
params.help: Which private subnets the cache lives in. Empty until the estate provisions or imports an ElastiCache subnet group.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the cache's port.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Cache name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this cache holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this cache.

decisions: Transit and at-rest encryption for a standalone Redis/Valkey node are engineer decisions — Memcached supports neither
Parameter group tuning and a non-default port or maintenance window are engineer decisions
Cross-AZ node placement (preferred_availability_zones) beyond the default is an engineer decision
